title: Comments in PHP
description: Document your code using single-line and multi-line comments.

slides.title: Why Comment?
slides.content:
# Comments Are for Humans

Comments explain what your code does. They are ignored by PHP.

*   Help others (and future you) understand your logic.
*   Temporarily disable code.

PHP supports two styles: single-line and multi-line.

slides.title: Single-Line Comments
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
// This is a single-line comment
echo "Hello"; # This also works
// echo "This won't run";
?>

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments
slides.content:
Enclosed between `/*` and `*/`.

```php
<?php
/*
   This is a multi-line comment.
   It can span several lines.
*/
echo "World";
?>
```
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
/*
   This is a multi-line comment.
   It can span several lines.
*/
echo "World";
?>

quizData.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid comment in PHP?
quizData.explanation: `<!-- -->` is an HTML comment, not a PHP comment. PHP comments use //, #, or /* */.
